framework_selection.reasoning: Paper uses TensorFlow 1.x (tf.gradients). Modern reimplementation defaults to PyTorch: torch.autograd.grad provides identical semantics for computing u_t, u_x, u_xx from the network output. torch.optim.LBFGS is natively available. PyTorch is also the dominant framework in Adi's existing DL environment (RTX 3050, CUDA 12.1, PyTorch 2.5.1).
framework_selection.config_management: YAML + argparse (lightweight; no Hydra needed for this paper scale)

src/pinns/models/mlp.py.role: Core fully-connected MLP used by both continuous and discrete PINNs

classes.name: MLP
classes.description: Configurable depth/width feedforward network with tanh activations. Serves as the shared backbone for SolutionNetwork_u and RK_SolutionNetwork.
classes.constructor_args: input_dim: int, output_dim: int, hidden_layers: int, hidden_neurons: int, activation: str = 'tanh'
classes.notes: Xavier uniform init applied in __init__. output_dim=1 for continuous (Burgers), output_dim=2 for complex (Schrodinger), output_dim=q+1 for discrete RK.

src/pinns/models/continuous_pinn.py.role: Continuous-time PINN: wraps MLP and computes PDE residuals via autograd

classes.name: ContinuousPINN
classes.description: Wraps an MLP as u(t,x). Exposes u_pred() and f_pred() where f is the PDE residual computed via torch.autograd.grad. The PDE operator is pluggable via a callable.
classes.constructor_args: hidden_layers: int, hidden_neurons: int, pde_operator: Callable, output_dim: int = 1
public_methods: forward(t: Tensor[N,1], x: Tensor[N,1]) -> Tensor[N, output_dim]  # u(t,x)
residual(t: Tensor[Nf,1], x: Tensor[Nf,1]) -> Tensor[Nf, output_dim]  # f(t,x) = u_t + N[u]
predict(t: Tensor, x: Tensor) -> Tensor  # inference only, no grad
classes.notes: t and x must have requires_grad=True when passed to residual(). pde_operator receives (u, u_t, u_x, u_xx, ...) and returns the residual tensor.

src/pinns/models/discrete_pinn.py.role: Discrete-time PINN with implicit Runge-Kutta integration

classes.name: DiscretePINN
classes.description: Multi-output MLP that outputs q+1 quantities (q RK stage solutions + u^{n+1}). The RK tableau is pre-computed at init and stored as buffers (non-trainable). The loss function uses the tableau to enforce RK equations.
classes.constructor_args: hidden_layers: int, hidden_neurons: int, q: int, pde_operator: Callable
public_methods: compute_un(x: Tensor) -> Tensor[N, q+1]  # applies RK equations (9) to get u^n_i
predict_next(x: Tensor) -> Tensor[N,1]  # returns u^{n+1}(x) slice
classes.notes: Gauss-Legendre nodes/weights computed via numpy.polynomial.legendre.leggauss(q) scaled to [0,1]. Stored as self.register_buffer('A', ...) and self.register_buffer('b', ...). IRK tableau computation is in utils/rk_tableau.py.

src/pinns/pde/operators.py.role: PDE residual operators for each example equation

classes.name: BurgersOperator
classes.description: Returns f = u_t + u*u_x - (0.01/pi)*u_xx. Takes u and its autograd derivatives as input.

classes.name: SchrodingerOperator
classes.description: Returns f = i*h_t + 0.5*h_xx + |h|^2*h. Operates on real/imag pair. Returns complex residual as [Re(f), Im(f)].

src/pinns/data/sampling.py.role: Collocation point sampling and dataset construction

classes.name: LatinHypercubeSampler
classes.description: Generates LHS samples over a rectangular domain [t_min, t_max] x [x_min, x_max]. Returns torch Tensors.
public_methods: sample(n: int, domain: dict) -> Tuple[Tensor[n,1], Tensor[n,1]]  # returns (t_f, x_f)

classes.name: PDEDataset
classes.description: Holds IC/BC data points (t_u, x_u, u_data) and collocation points (t_f, x_f). Used by the Trainer.

classes.name: BurgersExactSolution
classes.description: Generates the exact Burgers solution on a grid using scipy's spectral solver or a pre-saved numpy array. Used only for evaluation.

classes.name: SchrodingerSpectralSolution
classes.description: Loads pre-generated spectral reference data (256 Fourier modes, RK4 in time).

classes.name: AllenCahnSpectralSolution
classes.description: Loads pre-generated spectral reference data (512 Fourier modes).

classes.name: ContinuousPINNLoss
classes.description: Computes MSE_u + MSE_f for continuous-time PINN. Also handles Schrodinger variant with MSE_0 + MSE_b + MSE_f.
public_methods: forward(u_pred: Tensor, u_target: Tensor, f_pred: Tensor) -> Tensor  # scalar loss
forward_schrodinger(u_pred, u_target, h_bc_pred, h_bc_target, f_pred) -> Tensor

classes.name: DiscretePINNLoss
classes.description: Computes SSE_n + SSE_b for discrete-time PINN. SSE_n compares all q+1 RK-equation outputs against training snapshot data. SSE_b enforces BCs at boundaries.
public_methods: forward(un_pred: Tensor[Nn, q+1], un_data: Tensor[Nn,1], bc_pred: dict) -> Tensor

classes.name: Trainer
classes.description: Wraps model + optimizer + loss. Runs L-BFGS closure loop. Supports both continuous and discrete PINN modes. Logs loss at each iteration.
public_methods: train(max_iter: int = 50000) -> dict  # returns loss history
classes.notes: L-BFGS requires a closure that zeroes grad, computes loss, and calls loss.backward(). Full batch only — no DataLoader needed.

classes.name: RelativeL2Error
classes.description: Computes ||u_pred - u_exact||_2 / ||u_exact||_2 over the full spatio-temporal grid.

classes.name: GaussLegendreTableau
classes.description: Computes the Butcher tableau {A, b, c} for q-stage Gauss-Legendre implicit RK. Uses numpy.polynomial.legendre.leggauss for node/weight computation, then solves for A via Vandermonde system.
public_methods: compute(q: int) -> Tuple[ndarray, ndarray, ndarray]  # returns (A [q,q], b [q], c [q])
classes.notes: This is the most technically non-trivial component. Confidence 0.82 per SIR. Nodes c_i are on [0,1] (shifted from [-1,1]). A is the q×q coefficient matrix satisfying the collocation conditions. Reference: Iserles 2009, Chapter 4.

src/pinns/utils/autograd_helpers.py.role: Convenience wrappers for computing PDE derivatives via autograd

classes.name: DerivativeComputer
classes.description: Stateless helper that computes first and second-order partial derivatives of a scalar field u w.r.t. t and x using torch.autograd.grad with create_graph=True.
public_methods: compute(u: Tensor, t: Tensor, x: Tensor) -> dict  # returns {'u_t', 'u_x', 'u_xx', 'u_tt'}

classes.name: Config
classes.description: Loads YAML config, validates required fields, exposes as dot-accessible namespace.

data/generate_reference.py.role: Generates spectral reference solutions for Schrodinger and Allen-Cahn

continuous_pinn_forward.description: Forward pass for continuous-time PINN (Burgers example)
steps: INPUT: t_u [Nu, 1] float32, x_u [Nu, 1] float32  (IC/BC coords, no grad needed)
INPUT: t_f [Nf, 1] float32, x_f [Nf, 1] float32  (collocation, requires_grad=True)
STEP 1 — u prediction:  cat([t_u, x_u], dim=-1) → [Nu, 2] → MLP → u_pred [Nu, 1]
STEP 2 — residual inputs: cat([t_f, x_f], dim=-1) → [Nf, 2] → MLP → u_f [Nf, 1]
STEP 3 — u_t: autograd.grad(u_f, t_f, create_graph=True) → u_t [Nf, 1]
STEP 4 — u_x: autograd.grad(u_f, x_f, create_graph=True) → u_x [Nf, 1]
STEP 5 — u_xx: autograd.grad(u_x, x_f, create_graph=True) → u_xx [Nf, 1]
STEP 6 — BurgersOperator(u_f, {u_t, u_x, u_xx}) → f_pred [Nf, 1]  = u_t + u_f*u_x - (0.01/pi)*u_xx
OUTPUT: u_pred [Nu, 1], f_pred [Nf, 1]
LOSS: MSE_u = mean(|u_pred - u_data|^2)  +  MSE_f = mean(|f_pred|^2)
INPUT: t_0 [N0,1], x_0 [N0,1] (IC data), t_b [Nb,1] (boundary collocation), t_f [Nf,1], x_f [Nf,1]
STEP 1 — IC prediction: cat([t_0, x_0]) → MLP(output_dim=2) → h_pred [N0, 2]  (u_real, u_imag)
STEP 2 — BC evaluation: evaluate MLP at (t_b, x=-5) and (t_b, x=5) → h_left [Nb,2], h_right [Nb,2]
STEP 3 — BC derivative: autograd.grad to get hx_left [Nb,2], hx_right [Nb,2]
STEP 4 — Residual: SchrodingerOperator on (t_f, x_f) → f_pred [Nf, 2]  (real/imag parts)
LOSS: MSE_0 + MSE_b + MSE_f where MSE_b = mean(|h_left - h_right|^2 + |hx_left - hx_right|^2)

discrete_pinn_forward.description: Forward pass for discrete-time PINN (Burgers, q=500 stages)
steps: INPUT: x_n [Nn, 1]  (spatial coords of training snapshot at t^n)
INPUT: u_n_data [Nn, 1]  (known solution values at t^n, used in loss)
STEP 1 — Stage solutions: MLP(input_dim=1, output_dim=q+1)(x_n) → stages [Nn, q+1]
stages[:, q]   = u^{n+1}(x)
STEP 2 — Compute N[u^{n+cj}] for each j: apply BurgersOperator w.r.t. x only (no t) → N_stages [Nn, q]
Note: u_x computed via autograd.grad(stages[:,j], x_n), similarly u_xx
STEP 3 — Apply RK equations (9): for i = 1..q:  u^n_i = stages[:,i-1] + dt * sum_j(A[i,j] * N_stages[:,j])
Also: u^n_{q+1} = stages[:,q] + dt * sum_j(b[j] * N_stages[:,j])
Result: un_pred [Nn, q+1]
STEP 4 — Boundary enforcement: evaluate MLP at x=-1 and x=1 for all stage outputs
LOSS: SSE_n = sum_j( sum_i( |un_pred[i,j] - u_n_data[i]|^2 ) )  +  SSE_b

rationale.torch: Core DL framework; autograd for PDE derivatives, optim.LBFGS
rationale.numpy+scipy: Gauss-Legendre tableau computation (leggauss), spectral reference solutions
rationale.pyDOE2: Latin Hypercube Sampling for collocation points

cli_args: --config     PATH     Path to YAML config file (required)
--experiment STR      Experiment name: burgers_continuous | schrodinger | burgers_discrete | allen_cahn
--output_dir PATH     Where to save checkpoints and results (default: results/)
--seed       INT      Random seed override (default: from config)
--verbose    FLAG     Print loss at each L-BFGS iteration

evaluate.py.description: Load checkpoint and compute relative L2 error against reference solution
cli_args: --checkpoint    PATH  Path to saved model .pt file
--reference     PATH  Path to reference solution .h5 file
--output_dir    PATH  Where to save plots and metric JSON

data/generate_reference.py.description: Generate spectral reference solutions for Schrodinger and Allen-Cahn via scipy pseudo-spectral solver
cli_args: --modes  INT   Number of Fourier modes (default: 256 for Schrodinger, 512 for Allen-Cahn)

risk_assessment.description: The Gauss-Legendre implicit RK tableau construction is non-trivial. The SIR notes confidence 0.82. The A matrix satisfies collocation conditions involving a Vandermonde system — numerical conditioning at q=500 may be an issue. Nodes from leggauss are on [-1,1] and must be shifted to [0,1].
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use numpy.polynomial.legendre.leggauss(q) for nodes/weights; solve for A via the integral condition sum_j(A[i,j] * c_j^{k-1}) = c_i^k / k for k=1..q (Vandermonde). Use float64 for tableau computation regardless of model dtype. Cap at q<=100 for first validation; q=500 only after q=32 passes.

risk_assessment.component: DiscretePINN — N[u] computation over all q stages
risk_assessment.description: For each of the q RK stages, we need autograd derivatives of the MLP w.r.t. x. At q=500, this requires 500 separate backward passes through the MLP for u_x and u_xx. Memory and compute cost scales linearly with q.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Vectorize: evaluate all q stage outputs in parallel (MLP output is [Nn, q+1]). Use vmap (torch.vmap) or batch the derivative computation via jacobian. Alternatively, use the Jacobian trick: if x is [Nn,1] shared across stages, compute all stage derivatives in one vectorized backward call.

risk_assessment.description: PyTorch's L-BFGS implementation requires a closure and has subtle differences from scipy's L-BFGS-B (line search, termination). Results may not exactly match if hyperparameters are not tuned.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Use line_search_fn='strong_wolfe'. Set max_eval=max_iter*5. If divergence occurs, fall back to Adam for 1000 steps then switch to L-BFGS.

risk_assessment.description: Not specified in paper. Xavier uniform is assumed. Wrong init could cause slow convergence or failure for deep tanh networks.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement as config flag. Default Xavier uniform. If training stalls, try LeCun normal or small random normal (std=0.01). Design MLP class to accept init_fn argument.

risk_assessment.description: Paper uses Chebfun (MATLAB) for reference. We will use Python scipy pseudo-spectral solver instead. Small numerical differences may exist.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Implement generate_reference.py using scipy.fft + RK4 time integration with dt=pi/2*1e-6. Validate against known sech(x) initial condition. Accept <0.1% difference from paper's reference.

risk_assessment.component: create_graph=True in autograd for higher-order derivatives
risk_assessment.description: Computing u_xx requires two autograd passes both with create_graph=True to allow L-BFGS to differentiate through them. Forgetting retain_graph or create_graph causes silent errors.
risk_assessment.mitigation: Centralize all derivative computation in DerivativeComputer.compute(). Unit-test u_x and u_xx on a known function (e.g. sin(x)) before plugging into PINN.
